medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Female with history of HTN, dyslipidemia, CAD, CHF, PVD with known severe aortoceliac stenosis is admitted for severe abdominal pain. She has had chronic abdominal pain for past several months, which worsened over the last 24 hours. Described as diffuse across abdomen, occurring after dinner. Later, she had nausea/vomiting with 7 episodes, described as black, dark, and smelly. Constipation noted, with a large bowel movement last night that helped a little. On arrival, notable labs included WBC 20, Na 130 (chronic), hyperK, creat 1.2 (baseline 0.8). Denied recent fevers or chills. Additionally, reported an episode of substernal chest pain lasting 8 min, radiating to her arm, which has since resolved.
medical_history.past_medical_history: 1. Coronary Artery Disease (stress test showing lateral wall ischemia, EF 45%) 2. Congestive Heart Failure (flash pulmonary edema, uses oxygen overnight) 3. Peripheral Vascular Disease 4. Rheumatoid Arthritis (on prednisone, MTx, plaquenil) 5. T12 vertebral fracture s/p kyphoplasty 6. GERD 7. OP-T7 and T12 compression fractures 8. Anxiety 9. Microcytic Anemia 10. Chronic pain syndrome 11. Mitral regurgitation on catheterization 12. Tobacco abuse (quit 3 months ago) 13. LLL pneumonia 14. Hypertension 15. Dyslipidemia 16. Multiple intolerances to medications 17. Lap chole 18. Lap tubal ligation.
medical_history.family_history: No history of premature CAD or GI malignancy. Father died age not specified due to liver cirrhosis from alcohol. Mother died age not specified of bone cancer. Brother with PVD.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital signs: Temp 98.6, HR 106, BP 117/71, RR 16, Pulse ox 99% on room air. General examination notable for fatigue; abdominal examination showed diffuse tenderness.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, strength equal bilateral upper extremities, intact sensation, reflexes 2+. Cardiovascular: RRR, no murmurs, normal S1, S2. Respiratory: CTAB, no crackles or wheezes. Abdomen: soft, mild diffuse tenderness, no rebound tenderness, non-distended.
